emergencyBanner: In a life-threatening emergency, call Triple Zero (000) immediately
welcomeDescription: Ask me about any first aid situation. I'll provide step-by-step guidance based on The St John of God First Aid Manual.
disclaimer: Not a substitute for professional medical advice. Always call 000 in emergencies.

kbHandoffGuardianGuideHeading: Supporting mental health after a crisis
kbHandoffGuardianGuideBody: Guardian Guide connects you with trusted mental health and emotional support services.
kbHandoffCrisisCompassBody: Crisis Compass helps you navigate active emergencies with official guidance.
kbHandoffAidAngelBody: Aid Angel helps you find financial, housing, and recovery support after a crisis.
kbHandoffFirstAidAngelHeading: First aid when you need it
kbHandoffFirstAidAngelBody: First Aid Angel gives you step-by-step first aid guidance for emergencies.
kbHandoffLoveKeyBody: Connect with the Love Key HELP Network.
menuFooter: First Aid Angel — St John Australian First Aid 5th Edition
offlineBanner: You're offline — showing cached guidance. In an emergency call {number}.
mhfaHeading: Mental health is first aid too
mhfaHeadingWith: Mental health is first aid too — for {context}
mhfaBody: Psychological injury is now one of the largest categories of workplace harm in Australia. Panic attacks, burnout, suicidal thoughts, grief after a critical incident — these need a first aider as much as a sprain or a burn. Use the ALGEE action plan: Approach, Listen, Give support, Encourage professional help, Encourage other supports.
mhfaBodyExtra: Finding the right support — the right therapist, helpline, peer group or workplace EAP — is half the battle. We don't keep a list of crisis numbers here because they change and they're different for every person.
mhfaCta: Find the right mental health support at GuardianGuide.org
mhfaLifeDanger: GuardianGuide is our sister site for curating mental health resources matched to your situation. If life is in immediate danger, call {number}.
